<!-- Command: ensemble:discover-standards | Version: 1.0.0 -->
<!-- Description: Analyze codebase and extract coding conventions into a standards/ directory with index.yml -->

# ensemble:discover-standards

> **Mission:** Analyze a project codebase to extract coding conventions, naming patterns, error handling styles, test structures, and git commit practices. Write discovered standards to a standards/ directory with a machine-readable index.yml for token-efficient reference by future agent sessions. Always merge with existing standards rather than replacing them.

> **Constraints:**
> - DO NOT implement, build, or execute any application code
> - This command reads the codebase and writes to the standards/ directory ONLY
> - Never overwrite standards/index.yml without reading existing content first (merge-first)

## Phase 1: Codebase Scan

### Step 1: Tech Stack Detection

Identify the primary language, framework, test framework, and tooling

**Actions:**
1. Read package.json, Gemfile, requirements.txt, go.mod, mix.exs, *.csproj, or equivalent manifest files
2. Identify primary language(s) and framework(s) (e.g., Next.js 14, Rails 7, Phoenix 1.7, ASP.NET Core 8)
3. Detect test framework(s) from devDependencies, Gemfile, or test directory conventions
4. Detect linter/formatter config (.eslintrc, .prettierrc, rustfmt.toml, .rubocop.yml, .editorconfig)
5. Note monorepo structure if present (workspaces, packages/, apps/)
6. Print detected stack summary before proceeding

### Step 2: Convention Extraction

Sample representative source files to identify project-specific coding patterns

**Actions:**
1. Sample 5-10 representative source files from src/, lib/, app/, or equivalent
2. Detect naming conventions (camelCase vs snake_case, PascalCase for classes, file naming patterns)
3. Check for barrel exports, path aliases, and import ordering conventions
4. Identify error handling patterns (Result types, try/catch style, custom error classes)
5. Detect API patterns (REST routes, GraphQL resolvers, RPC definitions, controller conventions)
6. Identify test file naming conventions (*.spec.ts, *_test.go, *_spec.rb, test_*.py)
7. Run git log --oneline -20 to detect commit style (conventional commits, scope patterns)

### Step 3: Existing Standards Check

Read any previously discovered standards to enable merge rather than replace

**Actions:**
1. Check if standards/index.yml already exists
2. If standards/index.yml exists, read it fully and note all existing rule IDs and categories
3. Print "Existing standards found -- will merge" if the file is present
4. Print "No existing standards -- will create fresh index" if not present
5. Note the highest existing rule ID per category to avoid duplicate IDs on merge

## Phase 2: Standards Generation

### Step 1: Build Standards Index

Compile all discovered conventions into a structured YAML index

**Actions:**
1. Create standards/index.yml with top-level keys: version, generated, stack, standards
2. Under stack: set language, framework, test_framework, linter (use "none" if not found)
3. Under standards: create category keys -- naming, testing, imports, error_handling, git, api
4. For each rule entry use: id (category-NNN format, e.g., naming-001), rule (under 80 chars), example (optional, under 60 chars)
5. Assign sequential IDs within each category, continuing from existing highest ID when merging
6. Keep each rule under 80 characters -- this is a lookup table, not documentation
7. Merge any pre-existing rules from the previous index into the new output

### Step 2: Write Standards Files

Persist the index and expanded category files to the standards/ directory

**Actions:**
1. Create standards/ directory if it does not already exist
2. Write standards/index.yml with the compiled index content
3. For each category that has more than 3 rules, write standards/<category>.md with expanded explanations and examples
4. Print count on completion: "Standards discovered: N rules across M categories."
5. List every file written to standards/

### Step 3: CLAUDE.md Integration

Add a pointer to the standards index in CLAUDE.md if not already present

**Actions:**
1. Check if CLAUDE.md exists in the project root
2. If CLAUDE.md exists, scan it for any reference to standards/index.yml or /ensemble:discover-standards
3. If no reference found, append this block to CLAUDE.md: "## Standards\nSee `standards/index.yml` for coding conventions (auto-generated by /ensemble:discover-standards)."
4. If CLAUDE.md does not exist, print "No CLAUDE.md found -- run /ensemble:fold-prompt to create one."
5. If a reference already exists, print "CLAUDE.md already references standards -- skipping update."

## Phase 3: Report

### Step 1: Summary

Print a human-readable summary of all work performed

**Actions:**
1. Print the detected technology stack (language, framework, test framework, linter)
2. Print the total count of standards discovered per category
3. List all files written (standards/index.yml and any category .md files)
4. Print suggested next command -- "/ensemble:inject-standards <task-description>"
